Route Ready
Kits Guides Free Pricing Cheat Sheet The Book

Guides

Practical, no-fluff guides for route-based service businesses — pricing, contracts, and operations.

Cleaning

  • Commercial Cleaning Contract Template: What to Include

    A commercial cleaning contract template needs a task schedule, COI requirements, Net-15/30 terms, and a 30-day termination clause. Here's every section explained.

  • Residential Cleaning Contract Template: Free vs Paid

    A free residential cleaning contract template covers the basics; a paid one connects to your intake form, pricing, and cancellation policy. Here's when each makes sense.

  • Cleaning Business Client Intake Form: What to Ask First

    A cleaning business client intake form must capture property details, pets, access, priorities, lead source, and budget before you quote. Full field list inside.

  • Cleaning Business Contract Template: What It Must Cover

    A cleaning business contract template must cover scope, access, liability caps, cancellation, and your re-clean guarantee. Here's every clause, explained.

  • Cleaning Service Price List Template (Copy This Structure)

    A cleaning service price list works best as base prices by home size plus an add-on menu. Here's a copyable template and when to publish prices.

  • How Much to Charge for House Cleaning in 2026

    In 2026, solo house cleaners charge $40–75/hour and teams $50–90/hour in most US markets. Here's the flat-rate math that beats hourly quoting.

Pressure Washing

  • How Much to Charge to Pressure Wash a Driveway (2026)

    Charge $0.10–$0.35 per sq ft to pressure wash a driveway in 2026 — most standard driveways land at $100–$250, varying by market and condition.

  • Pressure Washing Contract Template: Clauses That Matter

    A pressure washing contract needs scope, water source, pre-existing damage photos, a fragile-surface waiver, weather terms, and payment terms.

Lawn Care

  • How Much to Charge for Mowing a Lawn in 2026

    Charge $40–$80 per cut for a typical suburban lawn in 2026, varying by lot size and market — and let route density, not the lawn, set your profit.

  • Lawn Care Contract Template: What to Include

    A lawn care contract needs scope tiers, billing structure (monthly vs per-cut), rain/skip policy, gate and pet clauses, and cancellation terms.

Running the Business

  • Route Density for a Local Service Business

    Route density — how close your jobs sit to each other — sets your real hourly rate. Two operators with identical prices can take home very different pay.

  • How to Price Service Business Jobs: The Formula

    Price service jobs in four steps: estimate hours from your production rate, multiply by a target hourly rate, round up, check your minimum job floor.

Templates are starting points, not legal advice. Have a local attorney review contracts before use.
